text: In-Context Learning (ICL) has become a core aspect of Large Language Models (LLMs) recently. Concretely, LLMs are used to perform a wide range of tasks by predicting the answers given a few few demonstration examples that are provided as part of the input prompt. Hence, exploiting ICL has become a way of both testing the hidden capabilities of LLMs and advancing them. This survey provides a complete overview of the current advancements as well as the open challenges related to ICL. Starting with the formal definition of ICL and some connections with well known machine learning ideas, this survey covers in details the optimization techniques for ICL including the design of loss functions, prompt engineering methods, as well as the design of scoring metrics. We also show the applications of ICL to a wide spectrum of use cases such as data engineering and data updating, as well as multi-modal applications like computer vision and speech recognition. Finally, this survey synopsizes the plethora of recent works that are trying to shed light on the underlying mechanisms of ICL. We conclude with the open challenges and suggest promising areas of research to advance ICL and bring LLMs to the next level.
